spec_paraphrase: ΔW = W_original − U·V; rank-r correction concentrates on important input directions
paper_verbatim: arg min_{W_hat} || W X - W_hat X ||_F
notes: Spec implicitly minimizes layer-wise compression loss via residual-correction widening; semantics match Eq.1.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not separately discuss naive SVD baseline (Eq.2 / ZeroQuant-V2)
paper_verbatim: arg min_{A,B} || Delta - A B^T ||_F
notes: Eq.2 is the baseline EoRA improves on; spec doesn't need to mention it.

spec_paraphrase: Eigendecompose A = QΛQ^T; Q' = Q_keep · √Λ_keep; ΔW' = ΔW · Q'; rank-r SVD on ΔW'
paper_verbatim: arg min_{A',B'} || Delta' - A' B'^T ||_F where Delta' = Delta Q' and Q' = U sqrt(Lambda)
notes: Spec's Q ↔ paper's U; spec's Λ ↔ paper's Lambda. Mathematics identical.

spec_paraphrase: V_corr = V'^T[:take_eff] · (√Λ_keep)⁻¹ · Q_keep^T (back-projection); widen U/V — algebraically equivalent to Ŵ·x + B'·A·x
paper_verbatim: X W_hat^T + X B^T A^T (forward pass with EoRA where B = B' Q'^{-1})
notes: Spec's V_corr correctly implements paper's B = V'^T Q'^{-1}: spec writes V_corr = V'^T · (√Λ)⁻¹ · Q^T; paper has B = V'^T · Q'^{-1} = V'^T · (sqrt(Lambda))^{-1} · U^T. These are identical (U=Q). Recent commit cf786a1 verification: notation aligns correctly.

spec_paraphrase: Residual ΔW = W_original − U·V appended via widening; equivalent to W_hat + A B^T forward
paper_verbatim: arg min_{A,B} || W X - (W_hat + A B^T) X ||_F
notes: Compensation objective implicit in spec's widening operation.

spec_paraphrase: Trace/Gram identity not spelled out in spec
paper_verbatim: arg min_{A,B} || Delta X - A B^T X ||_F = arg min_{A,B} [trace(...)]^{1/2}
notes: Internal proof step; spec correctly implements end result.

spec_paraphrase: Eigendecompose A = QΛQ^T; keep only n_keep eigenvectors above dtype-aware noise floor
paper_verbatim: By projecting the compression error Delta into the eigenspace with U sqrt(Lambda) as Delta' = Delta U sqrt(Lambda), minimizing Eq. 3 via SVD becomes equivalent to minimizing Eq. 6.
notes: D10 explicitly truncates to n_keep < k via noise-floor — weakens Theorem 1's exact equivalence claim. Justification is paper-internal (numerical stability for bf16); ch12-sanctioned.

spec_paraphrase: Proof step not in spec
paper_verbatim: trace((Delta - A B^T) U Lambda U^T (Delta - A B^T)^T)^{1/2}
notes: Proof internal; spec implements end algorithm.

spec_paraphrase: Proof step not in spec
paper_verbatim: trace((Delta U sqrt(Lambda) - A B^T U sqrt(Lambda))(...)^T)^{1/2}
notes: Proof internal.

spec_paraphrase: Eckart-Young invocation not explicit in spec
paper_verbatim: By the Eckart-Young theorem... SVD on Delta' is optimal for Eq.6
notes: SVD optimality assumed implicitly.

spec_paraphrase: Spec follows 7-step recipe: (1) ΔW=W−UV (residual against factored, not raw quantized); (2) eigh(A); (3) Q'=Q_keep·√Λ_keep with n_keep<k truncation; (4) ΔW'=ΔW·Q'; (5) rank-r SVD; (6) U_corr/V_corr back-projection; (7) widen factors
paper_verbatim: Algorithm 1: 7 steps with full Q ∈ R^{k×k}, no noise-floor truncation
notes: Two divergences from canonical Alg.1: (a) noise-floor truncation of eigenspace (sanctioned by D10); (b) residual is W_orig − U_old·V_old (against the Stage 3 factored form), not W − W_quant. The latter is the natural lift to a factored MoE pipeline (paper assumes pruned/quantized W_hat). Both are non-defeating: spec preserves √Λ weighting and back-projection per Eq.4.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 4 is purely linear-algebraic (eigh + SVD + matmul); no optimizer/gradients
paper_verbatim: EoRA—a novel, fine-tuning-free method... avoids backpropagation and completes in just a few minutes
notes: Spec has zero training step in Stage 4. Stage 5 KD is separate.

spec_paraphrase: Adds rank-r correction to existing factored representation by widening U and V
paper_verbatim: we attach residual low-rank paths... the weights of the compressed model are not modified during compensation
notes: Spec preserves residual structure; pre-existing factor (U_old, V_old) plays role of W_hat.

spec_paraphrase: Q' = Q_keep · √Λ_keep — full signal eigenspace, NOT truncated to r. The √Λ scaling importance-weights each direction by activation variance.
paper_verbatim: we propose projecting the compression error into the eigenspace... ensuring direct alignment between Eq.2 and Eq.1
notes: Core EoRA innovation correctly identified and implemented.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses input AUTO-covariance A = X^T X accumulated across calibration tokens (per Stage 2 §5: 'A_gate_up: input covariance for gate_proj/up_proj'); reused in Stage 4 as A = X̃X̃^T
paper_verbatim: X_tilde in R^{n x d} is the AVERAGE of the input activations over the task-specific calibration set. Following PCA, we perform eigendecomposition on X_tilde X_tilde^T
notes: Material deviation: paper averages activations FIRST then forms outer product (rank-1 X_tilde X_tilde^T using mean vector), spec accumulates X^T X (full second-moment, rank up to d). Spec's notation 'X̃X̃^T' visually matches paper but semantically differs. Spec's choice may actually be preferred (more information than mean-only), but it is NOT what the paper specifies and is not documented in §12. May be a paper-paraphrase typo in spec line 463 (X̃X̃^T) where the intended quantity is the second-moment matrix from Stage 2.

spec_paraphrase: √Λ scaling importance-weights each direction by activation variance
paper_verbatim: we leverage the eigenvalues of each activation channel as importance scores
notes: Correct interpretation.

spec_paraphrase: Back-projection V_corr = V'^T · (√Λ_keep)⁻¹ · Q_keep^T; n_keep restricted to eigenvalues above noise floor — implicitly handles Λ_i ≈ 0 case
paper_verbatim: Q'^{-1} = sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} U^T... no additional inference latency
notes: Spec's noise-floor truncation (D10) provides the numerical floor the paper hand-waves over with 'all Λ_i > 0'. Non-defeating; arguably more robust.

spec_paraphrase: For each (layer, expert, matrix); per-layer atomic checkpointing
paper_verbatim: EoRA compensation is applied to each compressed linear layer
notes: Spec iterates over MoE expert projections (gate/up/down) per layer.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not quantize EoRA factors
paper_verbatim: the low-rank matrices of EoRA are robust to quantization
notes: Spec's pipeline is BF16 throughout; EoRA factor quantization is an optional optimization not adopted.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not run downstream LoRA fine-tune from EoRA init
paper_verbatim: users can leverage low-rank matrices from EoRA as initialization for LoRA fine-tuning
notes: Stage 5 is router KD only; no LoRA path. Optional in paper.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 4 reuses Stage 2 covariance accumulated over 1024 sequences from nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-2-SFT-Data (multi-domain weighted: chat 0.56, math 0.21, science 0.11, ...)
paper_verbatim: We sample 128 concatenated sentences of length 2048 from the WikiText2 training set as the calibration set for EoRA
notes: Spec uses 1024 Nemotron sequences (8x larger, different domain mix); covariance reused from Stage 2 to avoid extra forward pass. Justification implicit in §2 (task-aware calibration matches deployment), but no §12 row covers this for Stage 4. D11 covers Router KD calibration (Stage 5) but not Stage 4. Recommend: add §12 row OR explicit note in §7 that Stage 4 inherits Stage 2's calibration choice.

spec_paraphrase: No task-specific calibration variants; single shared Nemotron mix
paper_verbatim: 32 ARC-train + 32 C4 sequences for ARC-C calibration
notes: Paper's per-task calibration is for benchmark optimization; spec's single deployment-distribution calibration is a different design choice consistent with §2 rationale.

spec_paraphrase: No task-specific calibration
paper_verbatim: 32 MathQA + 32 C4 sequences for math calibration
notes: Same as hp_calib_arc.

spec_paraphrase: compensation_budget_pct=3% of Stage 3 savings per matrix, capped at eigenspace_rank_cap=64 rank per expert
paper_verbatim: Rank of all the compensation methods is set to 128
notes: Spec caps EoRA rank at 64 per expert (half the paper default). No §12 row sanctions this. Justification likely budget/VRAM (256-expert MoE × 64-rank correction is already large), but uncovered. Note: 64 is in paper's ablation grid {64,128,256,512} (hp_rank_ablation), so it is a paper-acceptable value — but the paper's headline numbers all use 128. Recommend: add §12 row citing per-expert rank budget at 256-expert MoE scale, OR justify in §7 'Budget' subsection.

spec_paraphrase: rank cap = 64
notes: 64 is the lower bound of paper's ablation grid; gains scale with rank, so 64 sits at the conservative end.

spec_paraphrase: No calibration size ablation
paper_verbatim: calib ∈ {16,32,64,128,256,512} ablation; saturates ~128-256
notes: Spec uses 1024 (above saturation per paper Table 8).

spec_paraphrase: Single H200 GPU (141 GB)
paper_verbatim: single NVIDIA H100 GPU
notes: H200 ⊇ H100 capabilities; satisfies single-GPU constraint.

spec_paraphrase: Spec applies EoRA on top of SVD factorization (Stage 3 AA-SVD) of MoE experts, NOT on top of GPTQ/SparseGPT compressed weights
paper_verbatim: compressed using SparseGPT [6]... and GPTQ [1]... Channel-wise asymmetric quantization
notes: Spec generalizes EoRA from quantization/pruning compensation to SVD-rank-truncation compensation. The paper's framework Δ = W − W_hat is method-agnostic, so this is a natural application. Worth a §12 note clarifying scope.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not benchmark Act-S/ZeroQuant-V2/ApiQ alternatives
paper_verbatim: We compare EoRA with ZeroQuant-V2... Act-S... ApiQ
notes: Paper-internal comparison; spec adopts EoRA directly.

spec_paraphrase: No LoRA fine-tune path; Stage 5 is router-only KD
paper_verbatim: LoRA rank 128, 3 epochs, batch 64, lr 1e-5, cosine
notes: Optional in paper; not adopted.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 6 uses lm-evaluation-harness (per spec footer mentions lm-eval batch_size=auto:8)
paper_verbatim: lm-evaluation-harness framework
notes: Spec footer (line 763) confirms lm-eval usage.

spec_paraphrase: Spec mentions Act-S baseline as the degeneracy without √Λ scaling
paper_verbatim: Act-S = scale Delta by sqrt(mean |X_tilde|) per channel
notes: Spec correctly identifies Act-S as the baseline EoRA improves on (correctness note line 483).

paper_verbatim: Table 1 LLaMA3-8B 2:4 numbers
notes: Reference data; spec adopts the algorithm not specific benchmark numbers.

paper_verbatim: optimized CUDA kernel, accelerating inference by up to 1.4x
notes: Optional optimization; spec stays in BF16.

spec_paraphrase: EoRA factors not quantized in spec
paper_verbatim: quantizing 512-rank EoRA from 16-bit to 4-bit costs <0.5% ARC-C
notes: Optional in paper.

spec_paraphrase: V_corr has same shape as a standard right factor; widening preserves [d_out × d_in] equivalence
paper_verbatim: B' Q'^{-1} consolidated into single matrix with same dimensions as original B'
notes: Spec's widening produces standard FactoredExperts U·V; no eigenspace ops at inference time.

spec_paraphrase: Spec doesn't discuss SVD-LLM positive-definiteness contrast
paper_verbatim: EoRA employs eigendecomposition, only requires symmetry — naturally holds
notes: Related-work observation. Spec uses eigh consistent with this property.

spec_paraphrase: Computes residual ΔW = W_original − U·V (approximates compression error, not weights)
paper_verbatim: EoRA focuses on approximating the compression error itself
notes: Correct application of EoRA's residual paradigm.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 4 reuses Stage 2 covariance — no additional forward passes
paper_verbatim: EoRA finishes in ~15 minutes per LLaMA3-8B
notes: Spec amortizes calibration cost across stages; per-layer eigh+SVD is fast on H200.

spec_paraphrase: V_corr := V'^T · (√Λ_keep)⁻¹ · Q_keep^T (back-projected right factor)
paper_verbatim: B = V'^T Q'^{-1} = V'^T · sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} · U^T
notes: Notation verified per recent commit cf786a1: spec's V_corr correctly maps to paper's B. Spec line 485 explicitly calls out the (√Λ)⁻¹ term as critical — without it the correction operates in eigenspace not weight space. Q (spec) ↔ U (paper). All consistent.

spec_paraphrase: Original U_old/V_old preserved in widened form [U_old | U_corr] / [V_old; V_corr]; double-widen guard at line 716 prevents accidental re-application
paper_verbatim: weights of compressed model are not modified during compensation
notes: Stage 4 only appends columns/rows; the original Stage 3 factor block is bit-identical post-Stage-4.

spec_paraphrase: Cites +10.84pp ARC-C from paper as magnitude context; explicitly notes 3-bit experiment is not directly applicable to BF16 pipeline
paper_verbatim: 10.84% on ARC-Challenge, 6.74% on MathQA, and 11.45% on GSM8K for LLaMA3-8B compressed to 3-bit
notes: Honest citation; spec acknowledges scope mismatch (3-bit vs BF16+SVD).

spec_paraphrase: Not cited explicitly
paper_verbatim: +4.53/+3.48/+11.83 on ARC-C/MathQA/GSM8K for SparseGPT 2:4
notes: Reference data.

spec_paraphrase: Activation-variance-weighted importance over input directions
paper_verbatim: Inspired by PCA, eigenvalues as importance scores
notes: Conceptual match.

spec_paraphrase: Spec line 484 explicitly notes the SVD must operate on full [d_out × n_keep] (not pre-truncated) to preserve Eckart-Young joint optimality
paper_verbatim: By the Eckart-Young theorem, minimization of Frobenius norm achieved by SVD on Delta'
notes: Spec correctness note (line 484) shows clear understanding of EY optimality — calls out a previously-fixed bug where pre-truncating eliminates joint optimization.

paper_verbatim: we omit the subscript l
